operating_state: Total production increased to 1,902 MBOED in Q1 2024, driven by new wells across Lower 48, Alaska, Australia, Canada, China, Libya and Norway plus the Surmont acquisition. Organic production increased two percent year-over-year after adjusting for acquisitions and dispositions. Lower 48 operations reached 1,046 MBOED with strong Permian contribution while international operations continued ramping from recent project startups.
cost_structure: Production and operating expenses increased primarily due to higher volumes from Surmont acquisition plus increased lease operating and transportation costs. DD&A expenses rose driven by higher rates across segments and increased Canadian volumes. Total average realized price decreased to $56.60 per BOE from $60.86 per BOE in the prior year period.
material_events: Company returned $2.2 billion to shareholders through share repurchases and dividends. Retired $461 million of Notes at maturity in March 2024. Achieved 1,000th LNG cargo export milestone at Australia Pacific LNG in April 2024. Received license extension until 2045 on partner-operated Heidrun field in Norway.
macro_exposures: Henry Hub natural gas prices averaged $2.25 per MMBTU, down 35 percent from prior year due to mild winter weather and excess North American storage. Oil prices remained stable with Brent averaging $83.24 per barrel and WTI averaging $76.96 per barrel, supported by global demand growth and OPEC Plus production cuts. Bitumen realizations improved significantly to $44.30 per barrel from $29.49 due to narrowing WCS differentials.
forward_outlook: Second quarter 2024 production expected between 1.91 to 1.95 MMBOED with all full-year guidance unchanged. Capital expenditures expected between $11.0 billion to $11.5 billion for 2024. Company reconfirmed planned return of at least $9 billion to shareholders through three-tier framework. Declared second quarter ordinary dividend of $0.58 per share and VROC payment of $0.20 per share.
uncertainty_profile: Company faces ongoing commodity price volatility with earnings and cash flows dependent on crude oil, bitumen, natural gas, LNG and NGL prices driven by external market factors. Environmental accrual of $184 million maintained for remediation activities with 15 sites identified under CERCLA. Climate-related regulatory developments continue evolving with potential material impact on operations and compliance costs.
